Issue: document mentions an export function but does not provide a requirement
Correction: The 'Export Configuration' menu item opens a dialog where the current file can be saved by giving a name and the location.

Label: F11
Issue: does not specify what happens if the user tries to load a PCAP file while another is being processed
Correction: If a PCAP file is being processed, the system shall disable the load PCAP button

Label: F11
Issue: does not specify the format and size of the PCAP files
Correction: The system shall support PCAP files in the .pcap and .pcapng formats up to 1GB in size.

Label: F20
Issue: does not specify what happens if the (default) configuration values are invalid
Correction: If the default configuration values are invalid, the system shall display an error message and reset the values to 'none'.

Label: F20
Issue: does not specify the optimizer options
Correction: user can select between adam and adagrad

Label: F21a
Issue: does not mention how these changes affect the current configuration
Correction: Changes to default values also update all the used default values in the current configuration.

Label: F22
Issue: does not specify what happens if the preprocessor fails to run
Correction: If the preprocessor fails to run, the system shall display an error message indicating the failure and provide options to reconfigure the preprocessor or reload the PCAP file.

Label: F30
Issue: does not specify the maximum number of nodes and edges that can be displayed
Correction: The system shall support displaying up to 1000 nodes and 5000 edges in the network topology graph.

Label: F30
Issue: does not specify the default colors for protocols
Correction: The system shall use the following color-coding scheme for protocols: HTTP - Blue, FTP - Green, SSH - Red, etc.

Label: F30
Issue: does not account for networks with no communication if the PCAP is empty or irrelevant
Correction: In case the network topology is empty or there is no communication a message will be shown and the display will be empty.

Label: F30
Issue: does not specify if and how the nodes are enumerated or left unidentified
Correction: The nodes in the graph are enumerated by selecting any node with the minimum degree and applying bfs with this node at the root.

Label: F31
Issue: does not specify how the user is going to configure
Correction: The user can click a toggle switch to choose edge property.

Label: F40
Issue: does not specify the types of graphs used
Correction: Representation of the preprocessor is as an undirected graph

Label: F40
Issue: does not specify the maximum number of points that can be displayed in the preprocessor results graph
Correction: The system shall support displaying up to 10,000 points in the preprocessor results graph.

Label: F40
Issue: does not specify the update procedure
Correction: The user can update the results panel by clicking the update button

Label: F50
Issue: does not specify how the two graphs can be merged into one
Correction: The plots can be overlaid on top of the same graph, each value is converted to percentage to normalize the lines within 0 and 100.

Label: F50
Issue: does not specify the update procedure
Correction: The user can update the performance panel by clicking the update button

Label: F60
Issue: does not specify the zoom range for the graphs
Correction: The system shall support zoom levels from 10% to 500% for all graphs.

Label: F70
Issue: does not specify the image format for saving graphs
Correction: The system shall support saving graphs in PNG and JPEG formats.

Label: F72a
Issue: does not specify what happens if the user chooses not to save changes
Correction: If the user chooses not to save changes, the system shall close without saving any changes made during the session.

Label: F73
Issue: does not specify the file format for saving configuration
Correction: The system shall support saving configuration in JASON format.

Label: F80
Issue: does not specify the maximum number of runs that can be compared
Correction: The system shall support comparing up to 5 preprocessor runs simultaneously.
